title: Hugo Website Building Process
content:
Introduction Since I didn&rsquo;t want to create a separate blog for this tutorial, I&rsquo;ll use this article to explain everything in detail, ensuring you can successfully set it up as much as possible.
This article uses the Hugo framework and the hugo-theme-reimu theme. From creation and configuration to deployment, it&rsquo;s explained clearly in one article. For other themes, please refer to their official documentation.
Article Understanding and Configuration Instructions: To help you understand this article, I strongly recommend reading this explanation to understand which folder corresponds to which part of the tutorial below:
Folder or File Meaning /blog Initial folder /blog/blog Blog directory. If you customize it, please modify it accordingly, such as /blog/0721 /blog/blog/xxx Files or folders at the corresponding path in the blog directory /theme/hugo-theme-reimu/xxx Files or folders at the corresponding path within the theme folder Preliminary Notes: I am not the theme …

title: A personal observations and experiences
content:
A Personal Observation and Reflection Ciallo～(∠・ω< )⌒★
Introduction This is a purely personal note: about a name and emoticon I often encounter online—“Ciallo～(∠・ω< )⌒★”.
I’m not trying to conduct authoritative research, nor do I want to put it in the academic drawer; I simply want to write down my intuition, memories, and feelings as a kind of annotation on its existence.
My First Encounter with Ciallo I remember the first time I saw “Ciallo～(∠・ω< )⌒★” was in a fanfiction thread.
That line of text was like a gentle greeting, with a touch of playfulness and a hint of coquetry, yet without feeling pretentious.
It wasn’t a serious declaration, nor a joking mockery—more like a playful greeting between acquaintances.
So I stopped scrolling, wanting to know what kind of temperament lay behind this symbol.
Intuition About the Name The word “Ciallo” feels hybrid to me, carrying the outline of European colloquial speech while also possessing the casualness of internet neologisms.
Until I’m certain, I just want to pronounce it according to my own feeling: like the softer sound of “hi” and “ciao” overlapping in my mouth.
This softness contains intimacy, but also a self-protective, incomplete supply—it doesn’t reveal everything, leaving some room for the listener.
Closer Reading of Expressions Ciallo～(∠・ω< )⌒★ is like a mini-drama to me.
Each symbol is like a small gesture:
• The slanted brackets are like a slight tilt of the head;
• “ω” is like a slightly pouting mouth, with a touch of childlike cuteness;
• “<” is like the corner of a wink or a whistle;
• “⌒★” is like a closing smile and a small flash of light.
When strung together, it’s not a static expression, but a series of actions—someone performs a little trick in front of you, then smiles at you.
Feelings of Using It in Daily Life I started noticing this combination in different scenarios: sometimes it appears as a signature in comments; sometimes at the beginning of private messages, like a habitual greeting.
In these contexts, it doesn’t serve the core function of information transmission, but rather as a tone modulator: softening a sentence from bland to gentle, making a reply from stiff to warm.
It doesn’t take up as much space as a sticker, nor is it as laborious as a long narrative—it falls perfectly into the gaps of communication with warmth.
Meaning to Me Observing it as a pure symbol is ultimately cold; experiencing it as an expression of everyday emotion brings warmth.
For me, Ciallo～(∠・ω< )⌒★ is a cautious intimacy, a gentleness that doesn’t require much commitment.
In this digitally amplified and consumerized world, it’s like a miniature form of etiquette—no need to say anything grand, just set the tone first, and the rest will follow later.
Conclusion I like to focus on these tiny symbols because they reflect the subtle habits of human communication.
I’m writing this to remember the feeling of being gently greeted: unassuming, unobtrusive, like a little star winking at you.
Some might think this is just insignificant decoration, but to me, every act of kindness has its weight. It is these subtle acts of kindness that constitute the goodwill that is easily overlooked in everyday life.

title: Jigen Seiki——A simple anime/manga website
content:
Jigen Seiki - A Simple Anime/Manga Site If you can access the site successfully, it means it’s complete.
Phew~~
It took several days of fixing and rebuilding bit by bit. Sometimes the website wouldn’t connect due to broken links; sometimes images or CSS styles were missing…
Anyway, there were a lot of problems when we first started building it, and it took a lot of time, but it’s finally finished, just like you see now.
A simple anime/manga site of my own!!!
Notes Q&A Q: What is the name of this site?
A: Dimensional Starfield - A Simple Anime/Manga Site
Q: What is the name of the payment recipient on this site?
A: This site does not accept payments. If needed, please go to Dimensional Starfield Shop
Q: Where is the feedback channel on this site?
A: Please contact us via WeChat: Nino_Natsume0721 or email: info@natsume.xin for feedback.
Conclusion As this website is relatively new, its functions are still being improved. We apologize for any shortcomings!
